mnemonics: Set-Reset Latch: Set means output goes to 1, Reset means output goes to 0.
D-Latch: Data In, Data Out when enabled (like a door - open for data, closed to hold).

Flip-Flop vs. Latch: Flip-Flop is Edge-Triggered (like a camera taking a picture at the exact moment of a clock tick), Latch is Level-Sensitive (like holding a pose for the duration of a clock level).
keyPoints: Latches are level-sensitive and transparent while the enable signal is active.
Flip-flops are edge-triggered and only sample their input at a clock transition.
The basic building blocks are the SR latch, D latch, and the master-slave D flip-flop.
commonMistakes: Assuming a flip-flop's output changes immediately after its input changes, when it actually only changes on a clock edge.
Forgetting that an SR latch can enter an invalid or metastable state when both S and R are asserted (1,1).
